properties.property:
In the quiet hush where twilight falls, I hear the way your spirit calls. Your hand in mine, a perfect fit, A gentle flame, forever lit.
When I look into your shining eyes, I see the dawn of endless skies. The rushing world begins to fade, Within the sanctuary we have made.
Let seasons turn and years depart, You are the anchor of my heart. Through every storm and shifting sea, My soul belongs to you, eternally.

The coffee warms my hands, a nervous start, As twilight fades and evening sky appears. A sudden pull of gravity, my heart, Is drawn to you as every worry clears.
Tonight, the planets shine in glowing grace, With Venus chasing Jupiter above. Yet looking at the quiet, gentle space Between us, I've found a new trajectory of love.
The Eta Aquariids trace a silver line, From Halley's ancient, interstellar flight. A cosmic spark, a beautiful design, That brought us to this coffee shop tonight.
The universe is vast, the planets spin, But in this baggy sweater, sitting near, I feel a perfect orbit now begin, And thank the stars that you and i are here.

Two cups of coffee in the evening chill, The world slows down, the busy streets recede. The sky above is infinite and still, But your warm laughter is the light I need.
Like binary stars that share a gentle dance, Pulled by a gravity we can't ignore, I find a whole new cosmos in your glance, And suddenly, the earth holds something more.
The telescope might trace the Milky Way, Or seek the craters on a silver moon, But sitting by your side, I want to stay, And hope this evening doesn't end too soon.
Let galaxies expand and comets fly, Across the vastness of the dark and deep. Beneath this starry, overarching sky, This quiet moment is the one I'll keep.
